notes: This is a complex multi-series dual-axis chart with inset map and rare OCR of station names (Mauna Loa, ALOHA). All 15 model variants correctly identified the title, three data series, axis labels, and the inset map. About 10/15 correctly identified BOTH station names with correct marker shapes (ALOHA red dot, Mauna Loa red triangle), which is impressive given the small inset text. Common errors: (1) gemma variants often confused marker colors/shapes on the inset map; (2) qwen3-vl-8b erroneously claimed FOUR data series; (3) most variants slightly drift on ocean data start year (1985-1994 vs true ~1988-1990); (4) atmospheric CO2 2018 endpoints range from ~405 to ~420 (true ~415). pH endpoint values cluster around 8.05-8.08 (true ~8.06). The qwen3.6 family (27b and 35b) consistently delivered the most thorough and accurate responses with verbatim OCR and correct station markers. The nothink variants slightly outperformed think variants here, possibly because the task is descriptive rather than reasoning-heavy.
